steps.title: Spot the historical link
steps.content: The key signal: the Upland South was colonized largely by Scottish-descended people. This establishes Scotland as the source of the population that founded the Upland South.

steps.title: Infer the source of shared features
steps.content:
When an ancestral population and the populations it colonized share linguistic features, those features originated in the source.

Choice A states the Upland South acquired the “r” emphasis from Scottish dialects.

  • B imagines future spread
  • C reverses the direction
  • D contradicts the evidence
.

That matches choice A — A is correct.
